name: Tuvan
notes: Tuvan (тыва дыл) — Siberian Turkic language of the Tyva Republic, Russia. Written in a Cyrillic-based Tuvan alphabet (the Russian alphabet plus ң, ө, ү); a Latin alphabet was used 1930–1943. GRAPHEME INVENTORY NOT YET SPECIFIED: a reliable spec must model the Cyrillic palatalisation/iotation conventions, vowel harmony, phonemic vowel length (written by doubling) and the pharyngealised ('kargyraa') vowel series, which is not consistently marked in the orthography. INPUT CONTRACT: input is expected in the Cyrillic Tuvan orthography.
